# Phase 10.0.6 Meta-Governance Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, self-evolving governance systems, automated constitutional mutation systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Define how civilization safely evolves governance over extreme long horizons without creating doctrine chaos, institutional incoherence, governance fragmentation, runaway complexity, or constitutional instability.

## Scope

- Constitutional meta-governance doctrine.
- Governance evolution boundary philosophy.
- Anti-doctrine-explosion doctrine.
- Constitutional coherence preservation standards.
- Governance complexity containment doctrine.
- Institutional intelligibility preservation architecture.
- Governance mutation constraint framework.
- Constitutional simplification philosophy.
- Doctrine proliferation survivability standards.
- Governance scale containment doctrine.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No meta-governance or evolution layer creates runtime authority.
- No governance evolution bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No doctrine mutation layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No meta-governance doctrine weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows knowledge canon and doctrine indexing.
- Safe to include now: evolution boundaries, anti-sprawl doctrine, coherence standards, mutation gates, simplification philosophy, and scale containment.
- Future upgrade: doctrine change proposal, retirement, and merge review standards.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, self-evolving governance systems, automated constitutional mutation systems, or execution systems.
